title: Data Protection by Design (DPbD)
description: Personal data protection integrated into the entire lifecycle of a processing activity, from design through development, deployment to decommissioning. Aligned with the JPDP DPbD Guideline issued 30 April 2026 (PCP No. 2/2025 substantive content).

guidelineStatus.issuingAuthority: Personal Data Protection Commissioner Shariffah Rashidah Syed Othman
guidelineStatus.verificationNote: Verified against issued JPDP DPbD Guideline (April 2026, BM-only). Material divergences from PCP have been incorporated: (a) Cavoukian's 7 foundational principles condensed to 4 elements, (b) standalone children's privacy chapter REMOVED — children addressed only in Sections 5.3, 10.2, 11.2 with parental consent and accessible parent/guardian rights mechanisms, (c) prohibition on profiling/ADM of children for advertising/marketing NOT carried into issued text (PCP-only — leading practice), (d) no explicit citation of GDPR Article 25, ISO 27701, Cavoukian framework, or UN Convention, (e) new Section 13 'Amalan Terbaik bagi Tadbir Urus DPbD' (governance best practices), (f) new Lampiran A 18-item Data-Oriented + Process-Oriented checklist.

legalBasis.description: DPbD is not expressly referenced in Act 709 but its principles are inherently reflected in the 7 Personal Data Protection Principles (Sections 6–12). The DPbD Guideline operationalises Section 5 (mandatory PDP Principles compliance) by requiring data controllers to embed protection into the design of processing activities rather than retrofit it.

definition: Data protection by design means an approach that incorporates appropriate technical and organisational measures, which are designed to implement the Personal Data Protection Principles, into the entire lifecycle of a processing activity, from design, development, deployment to decommissioning.

scope.appliesWhen: All processing activities — DPbD is not triggered by a specific threshold; it is a continuous obligation across the lifecycle.

structure.principles: dpbd/principles.json — 4 elements of DPbD per Section 4 of the issued Guideline (NOT 7 Cavoukian principles)
structure.pdpPrincipleIntegration: dpbd/pdp-principle-integration.json — DPbD elements per PDP Principle (General, Notice, Disclosure, Security, Retention, Data Integrity, Access)
structure.lifecycle: dpbd/lifecycle.json — design → development → deployment → (operation analyst-added) → decommissioning. Note: the issued definition (§3.1) names only design, development, deployment, decommissioning; 'operation' is an analyst-added stage between deployment and decommissioning.
structure.childrensPrivacy: dpbd/childrens-privacy.json — children's content in the issued guideline is EMBEDDED in Sections 5.3, 10.2, 11.2 (NOT a stand-alone chapter as in PCP). File preserves PCP-derived analyst guidance separately from issued requirements.
structure.governance: dpbd/governance-best-practices.json — Section 13 Tadbir Urus best practices (board expertise/training, senior accountability assignment, performance KPIs, board reporting, DPO meetings)

internationalAlignmentNote: The issued JPDP DPbD Guideline does NOT explicitly cite GDPR Article 25, ISO 27701, Cavoukian framework, or UN Convention on the Rights of the Child. References below are analyst-supplied for cross-jurisdictional comparison only.

internationalAlignmentAnalyst.gdpr: Article 25 (Data protection by design and by default) — analyst reference, not cited in issued JPDP guideline
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.philippines: NPC Circular 2023-06 (Privacy by Design / Privacy by Default) — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.iso: ISO 27701 A.7.4 (Privacy by design and privacy by default) — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.ipcOntario: 7 foundational principles by Dr. Ann Cavoukian — historical influence on the field, NOT cited in the issued JPDP guideline

keyOperationalElements: Privacy as default (no action required from data subject)
Data minimisation at collection and throughout processing
Pseudonymisation and encryption as standard technical measures
Privacy embedded into system architecture, not bolted on
Full lifecycle protection — secure storage and secure destruction
Visibility and transparency — accountable to data subjects and regulator
User-centric design respecting data subject rights
evidenceExpectations: DPbD policy or standard signed off by senior management
DPbD checklist embedded in SDLC and procurement processes
DPIA records (cross-link with dpia/) for high-risk projects
Default settings configuration evidence (opt-in, minimum collection)
Retention automation evidence (auto-deletion at end of period)
Children's data processing inventory and age verification mechanism evidence (where applicable)
